description:
**Milestone:** Phase 2: Component Expansion

Create a ShadCN-style CLI that lets developers copy individual components into their projects (BYOS path 3). Uses @clack/prompts for interactive UI. Commands: init, add, diff, update.

**Files to Modify:**
- packages/cli/

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] helix init - initialize project with token config
- [ ] helix add <component> - copy component source into project
- [ ] helix diff <component> - show diff between local and upstream
- [ ] helix update <component> - update component preserving local changes
- [ ] Interactive component selector via @clack/prompts
- [ ] Respects project tsconfig and import paths
- [ ] Published as @helixds/cli

**Complexity:** large
title: Build @helix/cli tool

lastReviewFeedback:
coderabbitai: **Actionable comments posted: 7**

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for all review comments with AI agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

Inline comments:
In `@packages/cli/src/commands/add.ts`:
- Around line 9-17: Declare the variable `config` with an explicit `HelixConfig`
type to avoid implicit any: change the untyped `let config;` to a typed
declaration matching the return of `readConfig()`. Import or reference the
`HelixConfig` type from the module that exports it (e.g., where `readConfig` is
defined) and use it for `config` so the try/catch block and subsequent uses of
`config` are type-safe.
- Around line 96-97: The re-export of getComponentDescription from add.ts
creates an indirect dependency; either remove the export and update consumers to
import getComponentDescription directly from its original module (registry.js),
or keep it but add a brief comment above the line exporting
getComponentDescription explaining that this re-export intentionally
consolidates the public API surface for convenience (and document the canonical
source module). Locate the export statement "export { getComponentDescription }"
in add.ts and implement one of those two changes accordingly.

In `@packages/cli/src/commands/diff.ts`:
- Around line 55-79: The command currently uses a placeholder flow
(simulateDelay, spinner.stop, p.log.warn, p.outro) and must be replaced with
actual upstream fetch and diff logic: call the registry (using config.registry
and component) to fetch the upstream component source, decide which file(s) to
compare (e.g., localDir and target paths built with component), compute a
unified diff between local content and upstream content (use an existing diff
utility/import or library rather than simulated output), and surface real
results via the spinner and p.log/p.outro; update simulateDelay and the
hardcoded placeholder messages to proper error handling when the registry is
unreachable and ensur
